title: To Phillis.
content:
Phillis, you little rosy rake,
        That heart of yours I long to rifle;
    Come, give it me, and do not make
        So much ado about a trifle!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Thomas Moore, titled "To Phillis.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
